body: Waste Management demonstrated resilience in Q1 2026, achieving a 5.8% year-over-year growth in Operating EBITDA. The company's core collection
Q1 earnings results for major waste and recycling companies in 2026 ; WM's mixed Q1 led by strong core business trends, health care improvements.

**Waste Management, Inc.** reported solid first-quarter 2026 results, with operating revenues of $6,227 million, up from $6,018 million a year earlier. Income from operations rose to $1,113 million, and net income attributable to the company increased to $723 million, or $1.79 per diluted share, versus $637 million and $1.58 in 2025. Operating cash flow strengthened to $1,501 million, funding $650 million of capital expenditures, $354 million of share repurchases and $385 million of cash dividends. Total assets were $45,700 million and total debt was $22,891 million as of March 31, 2026, with stockholders’ equity of $10,021 million. Operating revenues: $6,227 million Income from operations: $1,113 million Net income attributable to WM: $723 million +5 more. Operating revenues $6,227 million Three months ended March 31, 2026. Income from operations $1,113 million Three months ended March 31, 2026. Net income attributable to WM $723 million Three months ended March 31, 2026. Operating cash flow $1,501 million Three months ended March 31, 2026.

Waste Management's Q1 2026 earnings showed strong EBITDA growth, driven by operational efficiency and strategic investments in renewable energy and recycling. The company expects volume growth in the second half of the year. Sustainability projects are on track.

Performance was driven by a disciplined approach to pricing and operational excellence, which allowed the company to expand collection and disposal margins by 110 basis points despite softer volumes.
 Management attributed volume softness primarily to severe winter weather on the East Coast and the lapping of significant wildfire-related volumes from the prior year.
 The sustainability segment is transitioning from a heavy investment phase to a 'harvest' period, with renewable energy EBITDA more than doubling following the completion of seven new facilities.
 Recycling profitability improved by 18% despite a 27% decline in single-stream commodity prices, credited to automation that reduced labor costs and improved material quality. [...] Management reaffirmed full-year financial guidance, expecting a volume inflection in the second half of the year as wildfire comparisons normalize and special waste pipelines remain strong.
 The health care solutions segment is expected to see a revenue growth inflection in 2026 as ERP systems stabilize and cross-selling synergies with the solid waste business manifest.
 Sustainability capital expenditures are on track to be substantially completed in 2026, with three additional renewable natural gas facilities expected to come online in the second quarter. [...] ### 2026 Outlook and Strategic Priorities

Positive Sentiment: The company delivered a strong quarter — Q1 Operating EBITDA grew nearly 6%, collection & disposal EBITDA rose ~6.4% with ~110 bps margin expansion, and free cash flow nearly doubled to $920 million, enabling ~$730 million returned to shareholders via dividends and buybacks.
   Positive Sentiment: Sustainability investments are producing returns — renewable natural gas EBITDA more than doubled after seven new RNG facilities went online and recycling EBITDA rose 18% from automation and higher processed volume. [...] 00:11:38

This growth includes the 20 basis point headwind John mentioned from the impact of higher fuel prices. Our recycling and renewable energy businesses together contributed approximately 50 basis points of margin expansion, reflecting accretive growth from investments in renewable natural gas facilities and recycling automation and new market projects. WM Healthcare Solutions contributed another 20 basis points of margin expansion due to effective cost management and synergy capture.

Image 44: David L. Reed

 David L. Reed

Executive Vice President and Chief Financial Officer at Waste Management

00:12:07

Following the U.S. EPA's increase of the renewable volume obligation, RIN prices have remained around $2.40, significantly above the $2 level assumed in the company's long-term investment outlook. Hemmer noted that about half of WM's long-term offtake will target the transportation market, with the other half directed to the voluntary market. [...] Core price reached 6.3% and yield stood at 3.9%, both surpassing internal expectations for the quarter. Core price growth exceeded 7.5% in both the commercial and landfill segments.

Pricing for single-stream recycled commodities dropped 27% during the quarter, yet operating EBITDA grew 18%, partly due to automation reducing labor costs and improving material quality. Chief Sustainability Officer Tara Hemmer reported that blended commodity value for recyclables was $69 per ton, near the company's anticipated $70 benchmark. [...] Collection and disposal volumes fell by 1.5%, with executives indicating that roughly half of that decline stemmed from severe winter conditions. Fish remarked that while weather impacts are typical annually, unusually harsh East Coast weather forced closures of several facilities for up to ten days, including some Stericycle sites. Intentional reduction of lower-margin residential accounts and comparisons to the prior year's elevated volumes from wildfire cleanup also contributed to the decrease. President John Morris noted that these declines were partly counterbalanced by a 2.7% rise in MSW volume and a 6.7% increase in special waste volume when excluding prior-year wildfire-related materials.

and renewable natural gas (“RNG”), which is a significant source of fuel that we allocate to our natural gas fleet. Our Healthcare Solutions segment provides regulated waste and compliance services (“RWCS”) and secure information destruction (“SID”) services in the U.S., Canada and Western Europe that protect people and brands, promote health and well-being and safeguard the environment. Additionally, through our Recycling Processing and Sales segment, we are a leading recycler in the U.S. and Canada, handling materials that include paper, cardboard, glass, plastic and metal. [...] Access to and Utilization of Credit Facilities and Commercial Paper Program [...] Order for the site cleanup in 2026. The recorded liability as of March 31, 2026, and December 31, 2025, was approximately $100 million. MIMC’s ultimate liability could be materially different from current estimates, including potential increases resulting from MIMC’s continued engagement with the EPA regarding the newly-approved remedial design for the site.

Effective Tax Rate: Approximately 18% in Q1, with expected full-year rate of 23% for 2026. [...] Growth Companies 20 NewProfitable predictable margin expandersStocks Sold w less CashThe Stalwarts [...] Operating EBITDA Growth: Nearly 6% increase compared to Q1 2025.
   Collection and Disposal Operating EBITDA Growth: 6.4% growth.
   Renewable Energy Operating EBITDA Growth: More than doubled due to new facilities.
   Recycling Operating EBITDA Growth: 18% increase despite a 27% decline in commodity pricing.
   Healthcare Solutions Operating EBITDA Growth: Nearly 12% growth.
   Free Cash Flow: $920 million, nearly doubling from the prior year.
   Shareholder Returns: $730 million returned through dividends and share repurchases.
   Operating Cash Flow: $1.5 billion, an increase of nearly $300 million from Q1 2025.
   Capital Expenditures: $650 million, including $61 million for sustainability investments.
   Leverage Ratio: 2.94 times, within the target range of 2.5 to 3 times.
